codeReview.summary: The fix satisfies the feature intent: the startup-behavior test now asserts the invariant that matters (the SSH tunnel to remote lightwalletd plus the dependency chain) while tolerating prepended reserved-port cleanup logic in `.factory/services.yaml`. I did not find any blocking correctness regressions in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `integration-worker` skill's step to "Add failing integration or end-to-end tests first" is too rigid for validator-repair tasks like this one, where the correct workflow is to reproduce and then repair an already-failing existing test rather than add a new failing test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/betterclever/newprojects/experiments/zimppy/.factory/skills/integration-worker/SKILL.md` step 3 says to add failing tests first, while the handoff at `/Users/betterclever/.factory/missions/6b54359c-94ff-48c3-8d41-8d0353f7b99b/handoffs/2026-03-19T06-51-20-020Z__protocol-core-startup-test-resilience-fix__dd2fcf0d-ce33-4bd1-978b-331d6f140a72.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: false` and explains that updating the existing failing startup-behavior test was the smallest correct change.

summary: Pass. The reviewed commit keeps the remote-only startup boundary checks intact while making the startup-behavior assertion resilient to additive pre-start safety logic in the service manifest. No blocking issues were identified in the implementation diff.
